name: Augustin
classification_notes: Augustin presents as a classical text serif with small caps and italics across two weights, suited to book and editorial typography in the humanist or old-style tradition.
summary: A two-weight text serif from Ludwig with full small caps and italics, pitched at book and literary typography.
description: Six styles cover Regular and Bold each with a matching italic and small caps cut, giving editors and book designers a compact but functional family for continuous reading. The specimen samples lean on literary and philosophical texts, suggesting a classical disposition suited to long-form setting. Without condensed or display variants it stays focused on the page rather than identity or headline work.
foundry_blurb: Ludwig present Augustin as a text serif available in Regular and Bold, each accompanied by an italic and small caps variant, making six styles in total. The specimen is demonstrated through literary extracts, positioning the family for book and editorial use.

name: Axiom
classification_notes: The presence of small caps, italics, and a text-focused optical size (Axiom 11) alongside a display size (Axiom 66) points to a classically structured serif family with transitional proportions and typographic thoroughness.
summary: A serif family in two optical sizes, one tuned for text and one for display, with small caps and italics throughout the weight range.
description: Axiom splits into two subfamilies: Axiom 11, intended for running text, and Axiom 66, scaled for display use. Both share weights from Light to Black, with italics at Light, Regular and Bold, and small caps at Light and Regular. The disciplined structure suits editorial and publishing work where optical size distinctions matter.
foundry_blurb: Axiom is offered by Ludwig as a family of 18 styles across two optically differentiated subfamilies, Axiom 11 and Axiom 66, ranging from Light to Black with italics and small caps variants included.

name: Aspen
classification_notes: The page content and specimen text suggest a refined, readable sans with a broad weight range and italics throughout, consistent with humanist sans conventions. No explicit classification is given by the foundry on this page.
summary: A humanist sans from Ludwig with nine weights and matching italics, built across a range from Thin to Black.
description: Eighteen styles span nine weights from Thin to Black, each paired with an italic, giving the family broad flexibility for editorial and branding applications. The specimen text ranges from short display phrases to extended paragraph setting, suggesting the design is intended to perform across both headline and text sizes. No condensed or optical variants are listed, keeping the family focused and consistent.
foundry_blurb: Ludwig present Aspen as a family of eighteen styles running from Thin to Black with matching italics throughout, suitable for a wide range of typographic applications.

name: Basta
classification_notes: The letterforms suggest a humanist serif with warm, readable proportions suited to text setting. The inclusion of small caps and italic cuts across multiple weights confirms a traditional text-serif approach.
summary: A humanist text serif from Ludwig with four weights, matching italics and small caps across the lighter cuts.
description: A text-oriented humanist serif with a nine-style family spanning Light, Regular, Bold and Black. Small caps are provided at Light and Regular, alongside italics at Light, Regular and Bold, giving the family good versatility for long-form editorial and book work. The heavier Black weight rounds out the range for display use without a matching italic.
foundry_blurb: Ludwig present Basta as a multi-weight text serif with nine styles, including italic and small caps variants at lighter weights, suitable for editorial and literary typesetting.

name: Brasil
classification_notes: The combination of small caps, multiple weights from Extralight to Black, and italic companions points to a classical text serif with transitional characteristics, suited to extended reading and editorial use.
summary: A text serif from Ludwig with six weights, matching italics and small caps cuts across most styles.
description: Brasil covers six weights from Extralight to Black, each with italic companions, and small caps variants at the lighter to medium end of the range. The inclusion of dedicated small caps styles suggests a design oriented towards extended text and literary typesetting. The weight range gives the family enough flexibility to work across body text and display sizes within editorial contexts.
foundry_blurb: Ludwig offer Brasil as a multi-weight serif family with 17 styles in total, spanning Extralight through Black with italics and small caps, suitable for both text and display work.

name: Brenta
classification_notes: The upright small caps variants, broad weight range from Thin to Black, and the overall page presentation suggest a classical transitional serif design suited to sustained text and editorial use.
summary: A transitional serif from Ludwig with eight weights, matching italics and small caps across the full range.
description: Twenty-four styles cover eight weights from Thin to Black, each accompanied by an italic and a small caps cut. The inclusion of small caps at every weight points to a design intended for considered typographic hierarchies in editorial or book contexts. The weight range is broad enough to span both delicate display work at the lighter end and forceful headline use at Black.
foundry_blurb: Brenta is presented by Ludwig as a serif family spanning 24 styles across eight weights from Thin to Black, with italic and small caps variants throughout the range.

name: Carree
classification_notes: Carree is a geometric display family with decorative variants including Panel and Contour cuts, placing it firmly in the display category with a modernist geometric character.
summary: A geometric display family from Ludwig with decorative Panel and Contour variants alongside standard weight cuts including a Wide companion.
description: Carree offers six distinct styles spanning standard weights from Light to Bold, a Wide variant for broader settings, and two ornamental cuts in Panel and Contour form. The geometric construction gives it a clean, modernist character suited to headlines and identity work. The decorative variants add depth to the family without departing from its underlying structural logic.
foundry_blurb: Carree is presented by Ludwig as a geometric display family available in six styles: Panel, Contour, Light, Light Wide, Regular and Bold, covering a range of expressive and functional applications.

name: Daisy
classification_notes: The presence of a Kursiv (italic) companion and the name Daisy suggest a text-oriented serif with traditional roman and italic styles; the pairing of a Regular and Kursiv points to a classical transitional or humanist serif structure.
summary: A serif typeface from Ludwig comprising a Regular and a Kursiv companion, suited to text and editorial setting.
description: Daisy pairs a roman with a Kursiv italic, following a classical serif model appropriate for body text and book work. The specimen phrases suggest a lyrical, literary register rather than corporate or display use. Limited information is available from the page, but the two-style structure keeps the family focused and purposeful.
foundry_blurb: Ludwig present Daisy in two styles, a Regular and a Kursiv, demonstrated through editorial specimen phrases such as 'light of my life' and 'Molecular cloud', suggesting a typeface intended for expressive text setting.

name: Contemporary Sans
classification_notes: The name and the organic, open letterforms visible across the weight range suggest a humanist sans-serif approach, balancing warmth with functional clarity across eight weights.
summary: A humanist sans from Ludwig spanning eight weights from Thin to Black, each with a matching italic.
description: Sixteen styles in total, covering Thin through Black with companion italics throughout. Suited to editorial and brand contexts where a clean, readable sans is required without strong stylistic personality.

name: Daphnescript
classification_notes: Daphnescript is a formal script typeface with flowing, calligraphic letterforms typical of classic penmanship traditions, designed for decorative and display use.
summary: A single-weight formal script with flowing calligraphic letterforms suited to display and decorative applications.
description: Daphnescript is a formal script typeface from Ludwig, presenting the fluid strokes and rhythmic consistency of classical penmanship. Only a Regular weight is offered, positioning it firmly as a display and titling face rather than a workhorse text font. Its character makes it well suited to packaging, invitations and editorial contexts that call for a refined handwritten feel.
foundry_blurb: Ludwig present Daphnescript as an elegant formal script typeface, shown in a single Regular weight across a range of literary and evocative display settings.

name: Daphne
classification_notes: Daphne appears to be a refined serif in the transitional vein, typical of Ludwig's output, combining classical stroke contrast with contemporary detailing.

The family covers core weights with matching italics, making it a functional choice for editorial and book typography. Its restrained approach keeps it versatile across both print and screen contexts.
foundry_blurb: Daphne is a serif typeface available from Ludwig, designed for refined text setting with a classical sensibility and a contemporary edge.

name: Dalli
classification_notes: Dalli is a script typeface presented in two variants — Script and Forte — suggesting a fluid, pen-influenced letterform family typical of casual or brush script styles.
summary: A two-variant script family from Ludwig, offering a lighter Script cut and a heavier Forte cut for expressive display use.
description: Dalli comes in two named variants — Script and Forte — giving designers a range from a flowing lighter style to a bolder, more emphatic weight within the same script idiom. The family suits editorial headlines, packaging and any setting where handwritten warmth is wanted at display sizes. The page demonstrates the faces across short phrases and extended prose, showing how both cuts carry rhythm across different text lengths.
foundry_blurb: Ludwig present Dalli as a script family with two distinct cuts: Dalli Script and Dalli Forte, shown across a range of editorial and display settings that demonstrate the expressive range of the design.

name: Essenz
classification_notes: The letterforms visible across the specimen suggest a humanist sans with organic stroke modulation and warm proportions, typical of text-focused humanist designs.
summary: A five-weight humanist sans from Ludwig with matching italics, designed for extended text setting.
description: Essenz offers ten styles across five weights from Light to Black, each with a paired italic. name: Diogenes
classification_notes: The upright and italic cuts with small caps across five weights suggest a classic text serif tradition; the proportions and weight range are consistent with a transitional or humanist serif intended for sustained reading.

That completeness makes it well suited to editorial and book work where hierarchy and long-form reading are both in play. The naming after the ancient philosopher suggests a classical disposition, though the page offers little further context about its origins or design intent.
foundry_blurb: Diogenes is presented across fifteen styles — Light, Regular, Medium, Bold and Black, each with an italic and small caps variant — suitable for text-heavy editorial and literary applications.

name: Diogenes Decorative
classification_notes: The name and foundry context suggest a highly stylised display typeface built on a serif skeleton, intended for headlines and decorative applications rather than continuous text.

name: Planet
classification_notes: The family's structure, with small caps, multiple weights and italics, points to a classical text serif tradition rooted in transitional design principles, balancing rational stroke contrast with humanist warmth.

classification_notes: The name and the four-style family structure suggest a serif designed specifically for screen readability, with Regular and Bold weights each paired with italics, pointing to a contemporary text serif optimised for digital environments.

name: Riga

summary: A six-weight humanist sans from Ludwig with matching italics across all cuts, suited to editorial and extended text settings.

name: Polo
classification_notes: The family's structure, with optical sizes, condensed and compressed widths, and a broad weight range, is consistent with a contemporary grotesque or neo-grotesque sans-serif. The naming conventions and specimen copy suggest a neutral, text-capable workhorse in the grotesque tradition.

Weights run from a hairline Fino through Extralight, Light, Regular, Medium, Bold and Black, with italics available across the standard and width variants. The scale of the system makes it well suited to publications and identity programmes that need consistent type across multiple contexts and sizes.
foundry_blurb: Ludwig present Polo as a broad, versatile sans-serif system encompassing multiple optical sizes, condensed and compressed widths, small caps, and a wide weight range from Fino to Black, with italic companions throughout.

name: Videon
classification_notes: Videon's name and sample phrases suggest a techno-geometric aesthetic; the clean, regular structure and weight range align with geometric sans-serif conventions, with a machine-influenced character.

The letterforms carry a techno-geometric quality without resorting to overt sci-fi affectation. No italics or condensed cuts are present, keeping the family focused and tight.
